title: Quiz: Trainability of Variational Circuits
description: Test your understanding of circuit trainability

questions.question: What does trainability measure in variational circuits?

options.text: How many parameters the circuit has

questions.explanation: Trainability measures how well gradient-based optimization can find good solutions. A trainable circuit has non-vanishing gradients that provide useful optimization directions.

questions.question: What is a barren plateau?

options.text: A region where the circuit runs slowly
A region where gradients vanish exponentially
A region with too many parameters
A region where qubits are unentangled

questions.explanation: A barren plateau is a region of the optimization landscape where gradients vanish exponentially with system size, making gradient-based optimization ineffective.

questions.question: What gradient variance threshold typically indicates good trainability?
questions.explanation: A gradient variance above 10^-4 typically indicates viable training. Below 10^-6 suggests poor trainability, and below 10^-8 is essentially untrainable.

questions.question: Deeper circuits always have better trainability than shallow circuits.
questions.explanation: False. Deeper circuits often have worse trainability due to barren plateaus. Shallow circuits typically maintain better gradient variance and are more trainable.

questions.question: Which strategy helps maintain trainability?

options.text: Using global cost functions on all qubits

questions.explanation: Local cost functions (acting on few qubits) have polynomial rather than exponential gradient variance scaling, helping maintain trainability.

questions.question: What is layerwise training?

options.text: Training one layer at a time while fixing others

questions.explanation: Layerwise training optimizes one layer at a time while keeping others fixed. This can avoid barren plateaus by ensuring each layer sees meaningful gradients.

questions.question: What is a good initialization strategy for avoiding barren plateaus?
questions.explanation: Initializing near identity (small values) keeps the circuit close to a known state initially, avoiding the exponentially flat regions that cause barren plateaus.

questions.question: Which factors can cause poor trainability? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Deep circuits, global cost functions, and random initialization all contribute to barren plateaus. Local observables actually help maintain trainability.
